title: Letter by Jamila Buzaglo about Eugene Delacroix drawing, 1832 (Solitreo script)

handwriting.notes: Letter written in Solitreo (Hebrew cursive script used for Judeo-Spanish/Ladino); full page of handwriting.

rights.evidence_text: Commons file page uses {{PD-old-100}}: This work is in the public domain in its country of origin and other countries and areas where the copyright term is the author's life plus 100 years or fewer. The letter dates to 1832 and the author (Jamila Buzaglo) died well before the public-domain threshold.

provenance.notes: Downloaded from Wikimedia Commons via upload.wikimedia.org. File page verified for license (PD-old-100). Image sourced from http://www.vocesdehaquetia.com/fotografias.html, courtesy of Centre de la Culture Judeo-Marocaine.

quality.notes: 712x618 pixel scan of a handwritten Solitreo letter. Legibility is moderate; individual characters are distinguishable.

title: Late 19th-century Rabbinic contract from Jerusalem written in Solitreo script

handwriting.notes: Rabbinic contract written in Solitreo (Hebrew cursive script). Likely Hebrew or Hebrew/Aramaic text as is typical for rabbinic legal documents.

rights.evidence_text: Commons file page uses {{PD-Art|PD-old-70}}: This work is a faithful photographic reproduction of a two-dimensional, public domain work of art. The photographic reproduction is in the public domain in its country of origin and other countries. The original work (late 19th-century rabbinic contract) is in the public domain as the author is unknown and the work is over 70 years old.

provenance.notes: Downloaded from Wikimedia Commons via upload.wikimedia.org. File page verified for license (PD-Art|PD-old-70). Sourced from https://www.jewishartifacteducation.com/sephardic-customs.

quality.notes: 1300x966 pixel scan of a Solitreo script rabbinic contract. Good resolution for HTR research on Solitreo script.
